title: Level 850
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE: Level 850: ''The Endless Stages''}}
{{Headerbox|file=850.5-1.jpg|header=The final challenge begins now.|speed=7s}} {{Audio|file=Countdown.ogg|}} {{Background|file=850.5-1.jpg}} {{Class 5|color-h=255|color-s=90%|color-l=15%|hexagon=E|entity=Hazardous phenomenon affecting the level|class=5e - Environmental}} '''Level 850''' is the 851st level of The Backrooms.

I - Description

{{Underline|FFFFFF}} {{Blurimage ALT|file=850.5-1.jpg||caption = The first image of Level 850.|border-color=red|text-shadow-Color=red|hovertext=red}} '''Level 850''', also known as the Theater of Endless Stages, is a presumably infinite hallway that connects numerous stages present in the level. Each stage has red curtains hiding it. After approximately 1 km, the hallway ends with a large black double door that is always unlocked and which serves as a transition between the hallways. Most of the hallways are identical to each other. However, it is possible to find slight changes between them, such as the primary color of the level changing to magenta instead of red, the floor coloring being inverted, the complete absence of curtains and the stages behind them, etc. The floor of the level is made out of concrete that is painted with a checkerboard pattern. Sometimes the paint has not dried completely, which can make paint stains on one's shoes, and it is possible to slip on it. The walls, on the other hand, are made out of painted drywall, which is anomalously indestructible. Rarely, black and white pictures of 50s theater plays can be found on the walls. Most of the pictures depict drama plays, although other plays can also be shown. Rarely is it possible to encounter a painting that depicts sections from class deadzone levels or one of the stages in '''Level 850'''. On the floor it is possible to find random items that are mostly related to theater plays. These items can range from small accessories to full costumes on mannequins. The costumes are usually old and dirty. Wearing them is impossible since they are glued to the mannequins. ---- {{Blurimage ALT|file=850.5-2.jpg||caption = The stage|border-color=red|text-shadow-Color=red|hovertext=red}} Behind the red curtains there is always a random stage. The stage can vary in size, shape, and other minor differences. All stages, however, have a few things in common such as being made out of any wood that can be found within [[The Frontrooms]], as well as stage lights with varying quality and functionality being present on every stage. Beyond the stages there is a dark void in place of audience seats and the rest of the theater room. The majority of the stages have a microphone lying on the floor; on most of the stages the microphone is broken, and even on stages when the microphone is fully functional, there is nowhere to plug it in. Several items can appear on the stages that can range from a few cardboard boxes to full speakers or musical instruments; these items only appear on larger stages. Curiously enough, papers, signs, and other objects have also been found that display such messages as "It's the end." or "Your judgement is nigh." It is currently a mystery as to what such messages could be referring to, although it has been speculated that these could be references to the exit to [[Level !]] (see below). ---- The void beyond the stages cannot be lit up by any means, which makes it impossible to see anything through it. The void can be considered as the most dangerous part of Level 850, as when staring into the void for more than 20 seconds, a sequence of events will occur no matter what. After said 20 seconds, the wanderer will start to feel anxious, similar to stage fright; due to the anxiety caused by the effect, it is difficult to forcefully jump into the void. Currently, it is unknown what happens when one jumps into the void, so it is not recommended to do so. Shortly after, the wanderer will exit the stage and will feel a massive income of adrenaline going through their body. After ~1 minute after the effect started, they will start running non-stop. Staying within the level longer will increase the pace of your speed. Once the wanderer goes through the black double door, they will end up in the hallways of [[Level !]]. Unfortunately, this method is currently the only known way out of this level. A unique side effect is that the sprinting effect will stay, allowing easier exits. However, depending on the speed of the sprinting effect it may not be fast enough to outpace the entities, condemning the wanderer to a painful death. It is possible to cancel the effect if you accidentally bump into a random object found inside the hallways; however, it may cause harm to the wanderer after bumping into something, depending on the speed and the object itself. At worst, the collision may result in some of the bones breaking or death.

II - Theories

{{Underline|FFFFFF}} ==

Affection by [[Phenomenon 15]]

== Currently the level has no reported entities. Furthermore, there has been no reported contact with another human being on the level. Currently this is just a theory, as there is no way of proving the theory, as that would require at least two people being sent onto the level, which would result in them getting sent to [[Level !]] and have a high risk of death in there.

== Replacement Theory ==

The earliest records of this level state that the exit does not lead to [[Level !]], but instead to a 'dark hallway of running'. This hypothetical level was supposedly consisting of tight stone corridors with almost no lighting. Other than notes found sparingly within the level itself, [[Phenomenon 99|there are no records of a similar level ever existing]]. While this has piqued interest in later years, evidence is sparse, and as such all beliefs are considered speculation.

III - Entities

{{Underline|FFFFFF}} As said above, currently there are no reported entities on this level.

IV - Colonies and Outposts

{{Underline|FFFFFF}} Due to the utter lack of resources on this level, possible affection by [[Phenomenon 15]], and the extremely hazardous exit, it is impossible to establish any permanent colony or outposts on this level.

V - Entrances and Exits

{{Underline|FFFFFF}} ===Entrances=== * Entering a movie theater on [[Level 369]] has approximately a ~8% chance to bring one here. * Getting below -150 karma on [[Level 995]] has a low chance (~20%) to send one here. * No-clipping through a stage in the backrooms has a slight chance to send one here. [ENTRANCE IS UNCONFIRMED] * Going through the door from a catwalk on [[Level 444]] has a small chance to take one here. [ENTRANCE IS UNCONFIRMED] ===Exits=== * To exit, one must stare into the void to eventually enter [[Level !]]. This is the only known way to exit this level. Note that if a wanderer has been in [[Level !]] before, then they will enter [[Level 144|Level 144]]. ... then they will enter [[Level 144|{{Wavy|Ḻ͖͑e̘͈̻̼̽̅v̺̥͑e͙̱͌̔l͍̲̭̹̉̏ ̹̔̑̌̀1̰̪̿͊̂4̞̹̺ͪ4ͥ͐.}}]] ... Note that if a wanderer has been in [[Level !]] before... Then they will enter ''what used to be''... ... Not today, though. Not today. {{Redirect|Prev=849|Next=851}} ---- {{Collapse|initial='''> Open Credits'''|final='''v Close Credits'''|body=
